executiveSummary:
## Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

**Enrollment and Spending Trends**

National Medicaid enrollment stands at 75.3 million beneficiaries as of January 2026, with most major states experiencing enrollment declines following the end of the public health emergency continuous coverage requirements. Texas maintains 4.0 million enrollees with a moderate 3.1% year-over-year decrease, performing better than several peer states including California and Illinois (both -6.2%) and Michigan (-7.6%). Only North Carolina among major states showed enrollment growth (+0.5%). Total national program expenditures reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, with a national per-enrollee cost of $2,869. Texas demonstrates relatively efficient spending at $2,988 per enrollee compared to high-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,377) and Massachusetts ($4,201), though it exceeds more efficient programs in Florida ($2,031) and North Carolina ($2,616). The federal government covers approximately 66% of total program costs nationally.

**Quality Performance and State Comparisons**

Quality measure performance reveals significant variation across states, with a national average score of 65.6 out of 100 in 2024. Texas ranks 30th nationally with a score of 62, placing it in the moderate performance tier but below the national average. This represents a concerning gap compared to top-performing states like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80), while Texas outperforms bottom-tier states such as Mississippi (48) and Oklahoma (51). Texas maintains strong managed care penetration at 93.3%, covering 3.9 million enrollees and exceeding the national average of 83.2%. Recent federal regulatory activity indicates increased oversight focus, with multiple temporary enrollment moratoria announcements and enhanced information collection requirements that may impact state operations and reporting burden in the coming fiscal year.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.03 million enrollees, representing a significant but controlled contraction of 3.1% year-over-year—tracking closely with peer state Pennsylvania (-3.1%) but performing better than large states like California and Illinois (both -6.2%). The state's per-enrollee expenditure of $2,988 sits just above the national average of $2,869, positioning Texas favorably among large states while maintaining fiscal discipline compared to expansion states like New York ($3,431) and Pennsylvania ($4,377). Notably, Texas significantly outperforms fellow non-expansion southern states, spending considerably more per enrollee than Florida ($2,031) and North Carolina ($2,616), suggesting a more robust benefit structure within its eligibility constraints.

However, Texas faces persistent quality challenges that demand attention. With a quality score of 62/100, Texas ranks 30th nationally—solidly in the "moderate" tier but trailing the national average of 65.6. While avoiding the bottom-tier performance of southern peers like Arkansas (54) and Alabama (52), Texas lags behind successful large states and represents a key improvement opportunity. The state's strength lies in its managed care infrastructure, with 93.3% penetration rate significantly exceeding the national average of 83.2%, indicating sophisticated delivery system organization. Moving forward, Texas should monitor how federal enrollment moratorium policies affect its non-expansion status and leverage its strong managed care foundation to drive quality improvements, particularly as procurement activities suggest ongoing system modernization efforts that could enhance both efficiency and outcomes.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 53% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,377 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,868, indicating potential cost control issues or unique population needs.

alerts.title: Florida Exceptionally Low Per-Enrollee Spending
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,031 is 29% below the national average despite having the 4th largest Medicaid population, raising questions about benefit adequacy.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critical Quality Performance
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi all scored below 55 on quality measures, significantly underperforming the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Texas: Moderate Quality Despite High Managed Care Penetration
alerts.description: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration (well above the 83.2% national average) but only achieved a quality score of 62, suggesting room for improvement in care coordination.

executiveInsights.title: CMS announces nationwide temporary enrollment moratorium affecting Texas
executiveInsights.summary: CMS has issued multiple Federal Register notices announcing temporary moratoriums on enrollment for Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP programs. These regulatory changes could directly impact Texas's 4 million enrollees and managed care operations.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment freezes would halt Texas Medicaid growth and require immediate system configuration changes and stakeholder communications. This represents a critical operational shift requiring immediate client notification and system adjustments.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag enrollment moratorium notices to HHSC leadership immediately

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score ranks 30th nationally at 62/100, below average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scored 62/100 on quality measures, ranking 30th out of 50 states and falling below the national average of 65.6. The state is in the 'moderate' tier with significant room for improvement compared to top performers scoring 78-81.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality scores expose Texas to federal scrutiny and potential corrective action plans. This creates opportunities for Accenture to propose quality improvement initiatives and demonstrates clear value-add areas for our engagement.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review quality improvement opportunities with HHSC quality team

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee spending at $2,988 vs national average $2,869
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $2,988 per enrollee compared to the national average of $2,869, representing a 4.2% premium. With 4 million enrollees, this translates to approximately $480 million in potential cost optimization opportunities.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher per-enrollee costs indicate inefficiencies that could be addressed through better managed care strategies or utilization management. This represents a significant cost reduction opportunity that Accenture could help capture.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review cost management strategies with HHSC finance team

executiveInsights.title: Texas managed care penetration at 93.3% exceeds national average of 83.2%
executiveInsights.summary: Texas has achieved 93.3% managed care penetration, significantly higher than the national average of 83.2%, with 3.86 million MC enrollees. This positions Texas as a leader in managed care adoption.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration demonstrates Texas's commitment to value-based care models and suggests opportunities for advanced managed care optimization rather than basic implementation. This validates our strategic focus areas.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review advanced managed care optimization opportunities

executiveInsights.title: New CMS benefit parameters for 2027 require Texas compliance planning
executiveInsights.summary: CMS has published the Notice of Benefit and Payment Parameters for 2027, establishing new requirements for Medicaid programs. These changes will require system updates and compliance modifications for Texas operations.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: New benefit parameters typically require significant system changes and compliance efforts, creating implementation work opportunities. Early preparation gives Accenture advantage in proposing compliance solutions to HHSC.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review 2027 benefit parameters for Texas compliance requirements

riskOpportunity.label: Quality measure improvement
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks #30 nationally with 62/100 quality score, creating pressure for enhanced performance management systems.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose comprehensive quality improvement program leveraging Accenture's analytics capabilities.

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline stabilization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.1% YoY, requiring system adjustments and potential cost management initiatives.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop predictive enrollment models to optimize resource allocation and system capacity planning.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care optimization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration above national average, presenting opportunities for advanced analytics and automation.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose AI-driven managed care optimization tools to enhance the high-penetration environment.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal regulatory compliance
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple new CMS regulations and enrollment moratoriums create complex compliance requirements for state systems.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory monitoring and rapid compliance implementation framework.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS procurement pipeline
riskOpportunity.description: Active Texas HHSC procurement opportunities create potential for contract expansion and new service offerings.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Actively monitor and prepare proposals for emerging HHSC procurement opportunities.

riskOpportunity.label: OIG oversight exposure
riskOpportunity.description: Active HHS OIG Medicaid portfolio oversight increases audit risk and compliance scrutiny for current operations.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Implement proactive audit readiness program with enhanced documentation and compliance monitoring.

riskOpportunity.label: Cost efficiency optimization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas per-enrollee costs at $2,988 vs national $2,869 average suggest opportunities for administrative efficiency improvements.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose cost reduction initiatives targeting administrative processes and technology optimization.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitive market positioning
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple states showing similar challenges create risk of competitor expansion but also reference opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop differentiated service offerings based on Texas-specific successes for national expansion.
